chapter_title.ar: باب مَا يَجُوزُ أَنْ يُؤَاجَرَ بِهِ الْأَرْضُ وَ مَا لَا يَجُوزُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 126 – What is allowed if you rent out the land and what is not allowed

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/340)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/340)

parts.text: أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي نَصْرٍ

text: الرَّجُلِ يَكُونُ لَهُ الْأَرْضُ عَلَيْهَا خَرَاجٌ مَعْلُومٌ وَ رُبَّمَا زَادَ وَ رُبَّمَا نَقَصَ فَيَدْفَعُهَا إِلَى رَجُلٍ عَلَى أَنْ يَكْفِيَهُ خَرَاجَهَا وَ يُعْطِيَهُ مِائَتَيْ دِرْهَمٍ فِي السَّنَةِ قَالَ لَا بَأْسَ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, and Sahl Bin Ziyad, altogether from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Abu Nasr, from Dawood Bin Sirham,

from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> regarding the man who happened to have some land for him upon which is a known taxation, and sometimes it tends to increase and sometimes decrease. So he hands it over to a man upon a stipulation that he would pay its taxation and give him two hundred Dirhams during the year’. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘There is no problem’.
en.sarwar: 5. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Sahl ibn Ziyad all from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ibn abu Nasr from Dawud ibn Sarhan who has said the following: “About the case of a man who has a piece of land for which he pays a certain amount of tax that may increase or decrease; and he gives it to another man to pay its taxes and two hundred dirham every year. He (the Imam) said, ‘It is not unlawful.’”
